title: Critoniopsis angusta
taxonomy: Critoniopsis angusta is classified within the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. Critoniopsis angusta is further classified within the subfamily Asteroideae. It belongs to the tribe Senecioneae. Critoniopsis angusta is a genus of flowering plants native to South America.

intro: Critoniopsis angusta, also known as narrow-leaved Critoniopsis, is a flowering plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. Bioactive constituents of Critoniopsis angusta include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Critoniopsis angusta is commonly used in traditional medicine, with herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts being made from its leaves and flowers to harness its medicinal benefits.
